message:
fix(share): extend symlink-safe validation to age crypto and deploy history paths

Follow-on to the recent symlink hardening work in the share crate
(04a78b7f "extend symlink-safe database path validation to bundle,
deploy, and finalize operations"). Two remaining surfaces were
still accepting symlinked paths silently and are now locked down
with the same three-tier validation pattern the crypto-adjacent
file paths elsewhere in the crate use.

## crypto.rs — age encrypt / decrypt

Three new private helpers are added:

- `require_real_crypto_file(path, label)` — `symlink_metadata`,
  reject symlinks, reject non-regular-files, and rewrite NotFound
  into a clearer "<label> not found: <path>" error.
- `validate_crypto_output_path(path, label)` — validate the parent
  directory exists (or is created component-by-component), reject
  any symlink in the parent chain, reject any non-directory parent
  component, and on the target path itself reject either a
  pre-existing regular file (returns AlreadyExists) or any
  pre-existing symlink/non-file (returns InvalidInput). Returns
  `Ok(())` only when the target path does not yet exist and the
  parent chain is entirely composed of real directories.
- `ensure_real_crypto_directory(path)` — used by the above to walk
  the parent chain component by component. Refuses
  `Component::ParentDir` to block `..` traversal attacks.

`encrypt_with_age` now calls `require_real_crypto_file` on the
input and `validate_crypto_output_path` on the derived output (the
input with an `.age` extension), before invoking the `age` binary.
The old "file already exists" check on the output path is
subsumed by `validate_crypto_output_path`. `decrypt_with_age`
additionally calls `require_real_crypto_file` on both the
encrypted input and the optional identity file path, and
`validate_crypto_output_path` on the output path.

Four new `#[cfg(unix)]` tests (using `std::os::unix::fs::symlink`)
verify each rejection path:

- encrypt rejects symlinked input
- encrypt rejects a symlinked output destination and does not
  clobber the pointed-to file
- decrypt refuses a pre-existing regular output file
- decrypt rejects a symlinked identity file

The encrypt test with the symlinked output also asserts the
pointed-to file's bytes are unchanged after the rejection so a
future regression cannot silently start following the symlink.

## deploy.rs — deploy history bundle directory

`record_deploy` previously used
`serde_json::to_string_pretty(&history).unwrap_or_else(|_|
"{}".to_string())` which silently wrote an empty JSON object on
any serialization failure. That path is unreachable in practice
but was masking the error type — it now returns
`ShareError::ManifestParse` with the full serde error so a real
regression surfaces the actual failure.

`write_text_file_if_absent_or_identical` and `read_text_file_if_regular`
now validate their parent directory against the new
`validate_real_directory_if_present` helper before any IO. The
helper walks the parent chain component-by-component, rejects
parent traversal, rejects symlinks anywhere in the chain, and
rejects non-directory components. When the directory does not
exist the helper returns `Ok(())` so the callers' existing
`ensure_real_directory` path can still create it from scratch.

Two new `#[cfg(unix)]` tests verify that `load_deploy_history`
and `record_deploy` both reject a symlinked bundle directory
that points to a real directory with a legitimate
`DEPLOY_HISTORY_FILE` inside. The write-side test also asserts
that the underlying real file is untouched after the rejection.

## finalize.rs

Pure rustfmt line-length tidy on one existing test — the error
assertion for the symlinked snapshot case is reflowed onto a
single `let err = ...;` line. No behavior change.
